quotes.quote: The Hub's vision is coming true.
I'm glad we had a fighter like you to help us take the spaceship.
We must finish our mission before the world is ground to dust under the rim of the great wheel of progress.
Dara asked me to bring any mercenary types to her first.
You definitely look like you know how to handle yourself.
Hubology has given me inner peace and expanded my mind. It can do the same for you.
Phil does the alignments that rid your body of polluting neurodynes. After we talk to Dara, you should go see him.
There are six of us right now, but I'm trying to recruit more members like yourself.
Dara says we need more people for the next step of her plan.
I've tried talking to the Raiders in the Nuka-World park, but they were not receptive to the potential benefits of Hubology.
Uh... I probably shouldn't have said that. Just forget what I said.
Back again? Our last talk didn't end so well.
Then you are not truly and completely happy.
I have been cleansed of neurodynes. I no longer need my birth name.
But you haven't answered my question. Are you truly happy?
They are kind of like ghosts that get into your brain.
They can cloud your thinking and hold you back from your full potential.
I can show you the path to true happiness.
The path to enlightenment through Hubology.
It can relieve you of your burdens. Help you find the true happiness you so desperately want.
It can also unlock the true potential of your mind, giving you great mental powers.
If you truly mean what you say, come with me.
I will introduce you to the others and you can take your first alignment.
The great wheel is crushing you and you don't even know it.
You are rim meat and it draws the neurodynes like carrion draws the bloatfly.
When your unhappiness becomes unbearable, I will be here.
The neurodynes infest your liver, colon and spine.
But they cannot be cut out with a knife, only through alignment.
When you are ready, come to me.
Hubology is the science of unlocking the full potential of the human mind.
It brings true happiness and peace to those that walk it's long path.
Dara is much better at explaining this. She's an AHS-9.
Come with me and she can answer all your questions.
That is her rank in the Great Wheel of Hubology.
I am but an AHS-6. She sits at the hub of the Great Wheel.
You can ask her all these questions yourself. Follow me.
I am the rim. The spoke and hub support me.
You should save your questions until you meet Dara.
Where are you going? We should stick together.
Keep your eyes peeled for threats.
There it is. Try to stay quiet.
I assume you are here for your next alignment.
You have advanced more quickly than any follower, even Cleansed.
This is the last mystery for you to learn. The last alignment.
There can be only one AHS-9, the highest you can be promoted is AHS-8.
The donation is 10,000 caps. The radiation dose will be severe.
Your donation is... lacking. Come back when you have the full amount.
It's understandable that you would be nervous.
Come back to me with a clear mind and healthy body.
What?! We've revealed our deepest mysteries and this is how you repay us?
I had such hopes for you. Now I have to kill you.
The Star Father can free our minds of all restraints.
We will be like gods, able to do unimaginable things.
Then we will return to Earth to rule over mankind.
It is a glorious task the Star Father has given us.
Yes. We will end the scourge of neurodynes once and for all.
The human race will reach it's full potential under our benevolent guidance.
I'll warm up the zeta ray projector.
Next step is to find the neurodynes and lock onto them.
It appears to be working. The neurodynes are fleeing or dying.
And it's done. You are now officially an AHS-8. The first one our group has ever had.
We cannot take you any further in Hubology.
Go forth and spread the word of Hubology throughout the Commonwealth.
We are forever in your debt. Without you, we would not be on the path to redemption. The path of our destiny.
You're more experienced at fighting than we are. How should we do this?
We'll wait for that signal. Good luck.
Really? Sounds dangerous, but we'll follow your plan.
You must be very confident in your skills.
It's your decision. We'll wait until you've killed most of them.
He is our best fighter, so I suppose that makes sense. Good luck.
Have you changed your mind about helping us capture the spaceship?
I can see the spaceship from here, just like he promised.
I knew I could count on you. Let's go tell the others.
Amazing! You truly were sent by the Hub. Let's go tell the others.
You mercenary types are all the same.
Go on. Leave. We'll find someone else to help us.
Your mind is limited by neurodynes.
If you had my expanded mental powers, you would see the truth about the spaceship.
The Star Father left instructions with The Hub for us to find the ship and repair it.
The Star Father will guide us to our new home on the planet Quetzal, where we will live with him.
It is central to Hubology. We are destined to make it fly.
More than that I cannot tell you. It is secret knowledge reserved for AHS-7 members.
Just sitting in that damned junkyard. So close. So tantalizing.
But I digress. Do you have the spacesuits?
Perfect! Here is your payment.
We can proceed with the next step in the plan. I'm counting on you to help us with it.
We need to take control of the spaceship in the Junkyard.
I know it's a lot to ask, so I'm willing to give you one of Hubology's great treasures if we succeed.
I guess that means you don't have the suits yet.
But I'm glad you want to join us.
Go see Phil. He'll give you your first alignment.
So no suits then? How disappointing.
It sounds like you can feel the repression of your neurodynes building up.
It's the reason I need those suits.
Without them we don't have a chance to capture the ship.
But enough of that for now. Do you have the spacesuits or not?
I see you brought me a new recruit. Thank you Cleansed.
Welcome stranger. We are hubologists. You may have heard of us. No? Ah, well.
The wheel turns and you return to us.
Have you changed your mind about helping us?
Normally new initiates go to Phil for alignment.
But I asked Cleansed to bring any mercenary types to me first.
I'd like to hire you to help us.
If you've come seeking answers to life's great questions, Hubology has them.
While I sincerely hope you'll join us, even if you don't I'm in need of a person of action.
Good. We need spacesuits, like the one I'm wearing. I'll pay you 200 caps for five of them.
I've learned that there are some in the Galactic Zone of Nuka-World.
I lost two followers trying to get them ourselves. I'm hoping you'll have more luck.
You haven't even heard my offer.
I'll pay you 200 caps to bring back five spacesuits from the Vault-Tec display in the Galactic Zone.
How sad that you aren't open to learning the obvious truths of Hubology.
But I don't need you to be a believer. I need you to get me five spacesuits from the Vault-Tec display in the Galactic Zone.
I'll pay you 200 caps if you do.
Before the great war, a brilliant writer named Dick Hubbell invented a process for expanding the human mind.
Through the process of alignment he was able to give his followers great mental powers.
I am his direct descendant and keeper of all his secrets, collectively know as Hubology.
If you really are interested in joining us, go speak to Phil about having an alignment.
We can help you find inner peace and happiness. We can help you master the amazing powers of the mind.
Hub's blessing! It was truly fated that we meet.
There is something else though. We need to take control of the spaceship in the junkyard. Will you help us?
Good. If it goes well, I might have more work for you.
For now though, just bring me those spacesuits from the Vault-Tec display in Nuka-World.
Too bad. If you change your mind, we'll be here.
Driving a hard bargain I see. Okay. 300 caps.
Alright, but 350 is my final offer. Now get me those suits.
You aren't the only merc in the Commonwealth. 200 caps. Take it or leave it.
I suppose there is no harm in telling you.
They will protect us in the battle to come.
That's not your concern. You just need to get them for me.
What a glorious battle. You were magnificent.
I promised you a payment. This gun was passed down from the Hub himself. Take great care with it.
Too bad about Tula. She died so young, before she could fully realize her true potential through Hubology.
I see the spaceship is already running too.
I need your help for one last thing.
I need to get that spaceship powered up again, but we don't have any fusion cores.
I have some extra ammo for The Hub's gun that I can give you if you succeed.
Good. Once you get it powered up, we can work on making it space worthy.
But the wheel will turn. You'll change your mind and come back to us.
The Star Father has promised me that it will work.
It may look like a thing of make believe, but we will make it work with the Star Father's blessing.
So will you at least get the power running?
It will carry us to our new home.
More than that I cannot tell you. You have not progressed far enough in your treatments.
We will take it to our new home on Quetzal where we will live with the Star Father.
It will be the fulfillment of The Hub's grand plan.
We fought a great battle today.
A battle we could not have won without our newest member.
A battle we could not have won without our friend.
But our work is not done yet. Taking the spaceship is not enough.
It must be made to fly again. It must be made ready to take us to our destiny.
Soon you will earn the greatest reward Hubology has too offer.
For now, rest and celebrate. You have earned it.
It came with a steep price though.
We will mourn Tula's passing. May she join us with the Sky Father.
The lights! It's amazing. You've done it.
We need to run a test of the system. Come inside with us and turn it on.
You'll see that the Hub's prophecy is true.
They may be a bit snug at first, but they'll protect you.
Everyone gather! Form the wheel.
The time has come. The Hub's grand plan can enter its next, important phase.
All of your hard work has born fruit.
We can liberate the junkyard and take control of what we need.
Thanks to our mercenary, we have the spacesuits to protect us from the robot guards.
Our new friend has liberated the junkyard.
So put on these spacesuits and take up your arms.
We have a mercenary to lead us into combat!
It is time for us to take control of it.
But first, here are the spacesuits I promised you.
It worked. It actually worked!
We couldn't have done it without you.
Here is the ammo I promised you as thanks for helping us to advance The Hub's grand plan.
We have a few months of work to do to prepare for our journey, maybe more.
Something's wrong. Shut it down! Shut it down!
When the time comes, we'll get a message to you to join us.
Don't take too long with those spacesuits.
We'll talk in a minute. First I must address the faithful.
The Hub would be proud of what we are about to do.
You look parched there, stranger. You know what you need? A bottle of Nuka-Cola Wild! The root-based flavor with teeth!
You ready to take folks their drink orders?
Good deal. Say hello to Prospector Owen, Miss Trixie, and Handy Hank for me.
Well don't wait too long. My customers ain't the most patient these days.
Appreciate your help, Shroud. You'll be delivering to Prospector Owen upstairs, and Miss Trixie and Handy Hank in town.
Appreciate it. You'll be delivering to Prospector Owen upstairs, and Miss Trixie and Handy Hank in town.
Employee manual login recognized. Exhibition bypass protocol engaged. Handing over part two of this week's safe combination.
That does sound like me... All right. Here's my part of the safe combination. Go get those outlaws, deputy!
Sorry, city-slicker, but you gotta go through with it. Ain't no backing out.
Hospitality is a big deal out here in Dry Rock Gulch. That's why I want you to handle tending the bar.
I got three orders of drinks I need you to deliver. One is just upstairs, and the other two are in town. You game?
Good job. Did Old Prospector Owen give you one of his great tall tales as a reward for his drink? What about Miss Trixie and Handy Hank?
What did folks have to say after you gave them their drinks? Did they tell you any exciting Wild West tales?
And, hey, why not take a few more bottles of Nuka-Cola Wild on your way out? Courtesy of Nuka-Cola!
Well, that ain't good. Nuka-Cola would like to apologize that this part of the Dry Rock Gulch experience was not up to our standards.
Rest assured, your complaint will be forwarded to the Human Resources and Maintenance departments.
Now why don't you take this part of the safe combination, and try out one of our other great activities?
Had one too many, huh? Ha. That sure sounds like the three of them, all right.
Well, you did a fine job. Here's my part of the safe combination. You can sling Nuka-Cola Wild in my saloon any time, partner!
I didn't realize all three of them were fans of the Silver Shroud. Learn something new every day.
Here's my part of the safe combination. Hope you teach those bandits in Mad Mulligan's Mine a lesson.
Now that's good to hear! You did a fine job, partner. Here's my part of the safe combination.
Why would I? Fine folks such as yourself are always on hand to serve them their drinks.
Now did Owen, Trixie, and Hank give you any of their fine tales?
Oh thank goodness, it's the new deputy. You gotta help me. My herd of Giddyup Buttercups have all fled the pokey!
Ready to help me corral my missing Giddyup Buttercups there, deputy?
Good luck out there. Remember, check the Nuka-Cola refreshment stands!
Well don't take too long, now.
Thank you, Shroud. Remember to check out the Nuka-Cola refreshment stands. Even heroes like yourself get thirsty.
Appreciate it. Remember, check the Nuka-Cola refreshment stands! That's where they usually run off to.
Employee manual login recognized. Exhibition bypass protocol engaged. Handing over part three of this week's safe combination.
Well, I suppose there ain't no harm in it. All right. Here's the third part of the safe combination.
And leave those poor Buttercups out of the stable? What if it rains?
You gotta track 'em down and bring 'em back to my corral. Now, Giddyup Buttercups can't resist the taste of Nuka-Cola, so check all of our refreshments stands!
What do you say, deputy. Can you help an honest horse-trader out?
If I ever need someone to tame these broncos again, I'll give you a holler.
Here's my part of the safe combination. Good luck in Mad Mulligan's Mine!
Oh no, what happened to Giddyup Buttercup! It was those darn outlaws, wasn't it? They butchered her up like a cow!
You can't let this stand, deputy! Take my part of the safe combination. You clear out those bandits from Mad Mulligan's Mine.
So you're the new deputy, huh? Well, I ain't impressed yet. Let's see how you handle an old-fashioned quick-draw duel.
You ready to slap iron and duel?
Scared, huh? I don't blame ya.
Well, Shroud, I hope for your ego, that you're right.
Let's see if your shooting is as fast as your mouth, partner.
Employee manual login recognized. Exhibition bypass protocol engaged. Handing over part one of this week's safe combination.
I suppose considering how crowded the park is today, that we can make an exception.
Fancy talk ain't gonna help you against outlaws, partner. You gotta show me you're ready.
The rules are simple. We'll each walk into the street. I'll count to three, and then I'll say "draw!" First one to hit the other wins.
Hey now, we can't have a shootout without you being properly equipped. Here. Take my spare pistol.
All right, gunslinger. Let's take this out to the street.
I count to three and then we draw.
Too slow, partner. Let me know when you're ready to try again.
Nice fisticuffs. Not exactly in the rules for a shootout, but I guess it showed you were faster than me.
Swingin' somethin' at my face ain't exactly in the rules... Wait, never mind, just don't hit me again...
Not sure if blowing me up should count... But I think you're too plum crazy for me to argue...
That's a fancy laser you got there. I guess I'll allow it...
Was that a plasma gun? All right. I'm just going to say you win.
That was some fancy shooting there, partner.
You've earned this. Hope you show those outlaws what a real deputy of Dry Rock Gulch can do.
And have a few extra rounds for that six-shooter I gave you.
Hold up, there. A shootout has rules. You gotta follow 'em. Let me know when you're ready to try again.
Hope y'all are having a good day here at Nuka-World. Ready to saddle up and ride into the old wild west?
Welcome back, partner! You ready for a rootin tootin good time here at Dry Rock Gulch?
The door to Mad Mulligan's Mine is locked up. I got a spare key in a safe by the theater, but wouldn't you know, I plum forgot what the combination to the safe was!
You'll need to talk to my three amigos: Doc Phosphate, One-Eyed Ike, and the Giddyup Kid.
Prove to them you're tough enough to take on the outlaws, and they'll give you their part of the combination.
Good luck, little doggie! And don't forget your complimentary deputy uniform, courtesy of Nuka-Cola!
Processing: Hostile visitor. Ignore and continue explanation for the sake of the other guests.
Employee manual login recognized. Currently running in full park exhibition mode.
Visitors are directed to our three main activity centers: Doc Phosphate's Saloon, Giddyup Kid's Corral, and One-Eyed Ike's quick-draw contest.
After completing each activity, the visitor will be given part of the combination to the safe that holds the key to Mad Mulligan's Mine.
Signs of life in the mine originally believed to be Mine Cart Ride attendants, but may actually be an infestation of deadly worms. Advise contracting exterminators.
System will now reboot. Have a nice day, and don't forget your uniform!
Processing: Confused or concerned visitor. No need to worry. I am just a friendly Nuka-Cola Protectron, programmed for theme park amusement.
Our friendly cowboy personalities will guide you and your loved ones through several fun, Wild West activities.
You must mean the no good outlaws that are coming out of Mad Mulligan's Mine!
Well, partner, if you're interested in lending a hand, I could use a new deputy! What do you say?
I'm the sheriff of these parts, and I need your help getting rid of those no good outlaws holed up in Mad Mulligan's Mine!
Well, now, we got ourselves a genuine celebrity! Howdy, there, Silver Shroud. We could sure use your help.
I don't know about this Bloodworm menace y'all are talkin' about, but we got ourselves a bunch of no-good outlaws holed up in Mad Mulligan's Mine.
I don't know about varmints, but we got us some no-good outlaws holed up in Mad Mulligan's Mine.
We could sure use a hand around here. What do you say? Want to be my deputy?
We like to say the only thing that's really in charge here is fun!
Did you have fun in Mad Mulligan's Mine? Tussle with any bad outlaws?
Did you have a rootin-tootin good time in Mad Mulligan's Mine?
I can't understand a word of what you're sayin', but you've been the best deputy this side of the Mississippi!
Processing: Emergency Defense Protocols activated! Intruder! Intruder!
It's been an honor having the Silver Shroud as my own deputy!
Well, all right. Please put in a good word with Nuka-Cola corporate when your survey card arrives in the mail.
I don't know what you're talking about. I'm just an old-fashioned cowboy trying to show folks a good time here at Nuka-World!
Here. Take some souvenir bottlecaps, courtesy of the Nuka-Cola Janitorial Service Department!
You and your friends are welcome in Dry Rock Gulch any time!
Whoa, there. You need to talk to one of the sheriffs first, partner!
Go talk to one of the sheriffs at the gates, first.
Talk to one of the sheriffs. The fun starts there!
Old Prospector Owen is just upstairs. Take him his drink.
Miss Trixie and Handy Hank are in town. Handy Hank is the blacksmith, and Miss Trixie lives right across from him.
Check all the Nuka-Cola refreshment stands. Those Giddyup Buttercups have got to be hiding around there.
Once you find a Buttercup, just place her in the corral here.
Howdy, partners! Welcome to Dry Rock Gulch, where it's always high noon!
Yeehaw! Get along little doggies at Dry Rock Gulch!
Do you have what it takes to be one of my deputies? Come on over and find out!
Feeling thirsty? Visit Doc Phosphate's Saloon for a free Nuka-Cola Wild! It's a rootin tootin good time!
Can't believe what those bandits did to my Giddyup Buttercup. It's a tragedy.
Thanks again for corralling that horse, deputy!
Hope you're having fun in Dry Rock Gulch, folks!
Don't forget to purchase a refreshing Nuka-Cola at any of our stands, partner!
There he is. The fastest gun in the West.
There she is. The fastest gun in the West.
I ain't got nothin' for ya. Go on now.
You're welcome at Doc Phosphate's any time.
Feel free to take any of the Nuka-Cola Wild samples we have in the saloon.
Please, just... don't hurt me.
=I'm not much for conversation.
Just working hard, like always.
Market's been real busy lately. Guess things are going well.
Got four good hours of sleep last night.
I don't care to talk too much. Gets me in trouble.
These collars get damn hot after a while.
Seems like every time I fix one thing, another thing breaks.
There's some bad jobs out here, but cleaning up the Pack's hideout is the worst.
Seems like the work never ends. But I'll get it done, somehow.
New boss, huh? Don't worry, I don't cause problems.
There's machines here that used to spray kids with Nuka-Cola. What was wrong with those people?
Got the lights back on, huh? Least I won't die in the dark.
Oh, uh... hey, boss. Didn't see you there.
Things will get better... one day...
Used to work because the Raiders made me. Now it's because I really want to make this place better.
The gangs are gone. It's like a weight lifted off us. Thanks.
You really gave those Raiders hell, boss.
Got the power plant back online, huh?
Never thought I'd live long enough to see the spaceship working.
That was quite a battle with the robot. Quite a battle.
You aren't so bad after all. I kept waiting for you to betray us, but you didn't.
Don't go bothering me. Dara is who you want to see. Wearing that suit of hers.
Another new recruit. We'll see if you've got what it takes to be a hubologist.
I've got my eye on you. New recruits have been know to try and steal from us.
I'll catch up to you. I'm too old to run like this.
Marvel at the animatronic models.
Come experience the wonder and horrors of the Grandchester Mystery Mansion.
Why did little Lucy gruesomely kill her parents?
See where she committed the bloody deed.
See the actual kitchen where her mother loving prepared her meals.
See the bloodstains that can't be washed out.
Not suitable for children under 5.
No animals were harmed in the making of this attraction.
Grandchester Mystery Mansion Inc. is not responsible for health risks of this attraction.
This includes but is not limited to: heart attack, stroke, fainting or severe cuts and bruises.
Enjoy your visit to the Grandchester Mystery Mansion.
Kicking or spray painting the animatronic models is prohibited.
Tickets may be purchased for sixty-five dollars.
Those without exact change can purchase a ticket from the cashier.
No admission without a ticket.
Invalid credentials. Employee status not confirmed.
You must purchase admission to the Grandchester Mystery Mansion.
Welcome to the Grandchester Mystery Mansion.
Inside you will learn the story of Lucy and how she gruesomely murdered her parents.
View the actual floors she walked on.
All for only sixty-five dollars.
The Grandchester Mystery Mansion only accepts legal tender.
If you do not have exact change, you can buy your ticket from the cashier.
Please move to the back of the line.
Understood. Please exit the line.
Thank you for visiting the Grandchester Mystery Mansion. Please enjoy your stay.
Remember to purchase a memento of your visit at the gift shop.
Your admission to the Grandchester Mystery Mansion is valid for up to ... days after admission. You have ... days remaining.
Please remember: Grandchester Mystery Mansion Inc. is not responsible for any injuries sustained during your visit.
Our newest member. Welcome AHS-1. Are you ready to do battle with your neurodynes?
You have just begun your journey toward enlightenment and true happiness.
Don't give up on it now. Come back soon.
I suppose we are friends. Okay, but this is the last time.
You do want to free your mind and find greater happiness, don't you?
The human mind has unlimited potential.
But neurodynes prevent us from reaching it.
The zeta radiation we administer in an alignment will destroy some of them.
The spirits of the dead are constantly trying to influence us.
They reach into our brains and alter our chemistry to create neurodynes.
Zeta radiation destroys some of the neurodynes and drives the spirits away, but only for a short time.
We ask for a modest commitment of 100 caps for this alignment.
You'll take a bit more rads than last time, so make sure you are healthy before taking this alignment.
You are doing well AHS-2. In your last alignment I saw unhealthy attachments to suppressors.
Very difficult to treat. However, you'll only need to donate 250 caps.
Of course the rad dosage is a bit higher too.
I worry about the influence of those suppressors. Don't wait too long.
Those are the people in your life that are suppressing your talents and abilities.
They are riddled with neurodynes like you used to be.
Their brain waves can trigger excess neurodyne activity in you.
I strongly recommend you avoid them.
Almost everyone in the Commonwealth is a suppressor, but some are worse than others.
Anyone that tried to convince you of the rightness of their cause is almost certainly a suppressor.
We usually recommend limiting your contact with people who are not hubologists.
Welcome back AHS-3. It is time for a fresh alignment.
We'll be treating more deeply rooted neurodynes. You'll need to donate 500 caps.
The radiation is also strong, so make sure your rads are low.
You are only an AHS-3. You are still highly susceptible to suppressors. Please be careful out there.
It's hard to say. Probably until The Hub's grand plan begins.
The details are restricted to AHS-6 and above.
But I can tell you that it will end the need for alignments. We will be forever protected from the neurodynes of malignant spirits.
It's good to see you AHS-4. You are progressing quickly.
I'm sure the Star Father is impressed.
We need to probe deeper, and you will have to take more rads.
It will take a donation of 1000 caps for this alignment.
You are doing so well! It breaks my heart to see you so close to true enlightenment only to be distracted. Come back soon.
Our founder, The Hub, made contact with the Star Father long ago.
He lives on the distant planet Quetzal in the constellation of Zyphus.
He is the source of all enlightenment through the alignments he taught The Hub.
The tools are manmade, but the process is a gift from the Star Father through his prophet, The Hub.
Is it time for another alignment already AHS-5?
You are about to be blessed by the Star Father.
Although if it were up to me, you would wait. You are rising too quickly.
This is an even deeper probe with even more rads than last time. A donation of 2500 caps will cover it.
If you must wait, wait here with us.
The outside world will bring you into contact with suppressive influences.
I'd hate to undo all the work we've accomplished so far.
He is the source of life on Earth. We are all his descendants.
But we have lost our way, led astray by the neurodynes.
But that will all end with The Hub's grand plan.
You are not ready for that mystery yet AHS-5. Maybe next time.
But rest assured, The Hub received guidance from the Star Father.
We all have a place in his grand plan.
I've told Dara that you are rising too quickly. Even Cleansed didn't promote this quickly.
He's been working for months to save enough caps for his next alignment.
But I'm sure you won't have any problems donating 5000 caps.
Then please sit in the Zeta Aligner.
This isn't enough. Come back when you have the caps.
You risk undoing all of your hard work so far.
Just stay away from suppressors and don't wait too long for your next alignment.
You are no better than the other tainted minds. You are destined to be nothing but rim meat.
Now that you are AHS-6, you are ready.
The Hub persuaded the Star Father to let the true faithful live with him on the planet Quetzal.
We need to show our devotion by constantly fighting the influences of the neurodynes and suppressors.
The Star Father provided the co-ordinates of Quetzal to The Hub.
But it's up to us to provide the means. Our hard work shows our commitment to him and our worthiness to sit by his side.
Dara has said your treatment must wait until we have achieved our goal.
Once we have control of the junkyard you can receive another alignment.
It's taken me years to master the rank of AHS-7.
Yet Dara refuses to promote me. Me! Her most loyal follower.
But you. You come in here and she fawns all over you.
She's going to replace me, I know it. Go on. Go to Dara. Only she can promote you to AHS-8.
Are you ready to throw off the shackles of your neurodynes?
If you let it go on too long, the neurodynes will just build up in you mind, clouding your abilities.
Have a seat in the Zeta Aligner.
That would not show the proper commitment on your part.
Neurodynes build up in your brain, clouding your ability to think.
After an alignment, you will think more clearly and be happier.
You are strapped into the chair and your central nervous system is pumped full of zeta radiation.
This destroys some of the neurodynes. However, over time, they will build up again without further treatment.
Hubology takes commitment. A small donation of 50 caps shows your commitment to self-improvement.
You will take some rads during the procedure, so make sure you are healthy before starting.
Let me start up the zeta rays.
Okay, locking onto the neurodynes.
There are so many of them! What have you been doing?
You seem to have gathered almost as many as I removed before.
Good. The zeta rays are withering them.
You really should be more careful about the company you keep.
The zeta rays are destroying them.
I like watching them squirm and die.
These are more active than normal.
25 percent... 50 percent... 75 percent... gone.

Weak. That ain't gonna happen to me.

Well, hey there. What brings you all the way out here?
Woah, okay. Take it easy. Just trying to break the ice, you know?
Hm. I suppose it's because I don't like to judge people until I get to know them.
Sorry if I caught you off guard.
The name's Evan. I've been out here for years now.
I guess you could say that I like the peace and quiet, and the view... well, look for yourself. It's something else.
Since you took the trouble to find me, the least I can do is return the favor. Need any supplies?
Glad to help. Let me see what I've got...
Nonsense. There's got to be something you could use...
No, no... I'm afraid that's a bit beyond my reach.
Let's just say I've been out there and I know that the world can be a cruel and unfriendly place.
So, is there anything I can help you with?
Here you go. I found this on the outskirts of Nuka-World a while back. Isn't doing me any good.
Well, it's been nice having a visitor.
Feel free to stay for a while and help yourself to anything else I have laying around the place.
Don't mind me... I'm just going to hang out around here and enjoy the view.
Good luck out there, stranger.
You're the new Overboss, right? You sure made an example out of Colter.
Well, you have to admit, you had a bit of help.
Well, judging from the Thirst Zapper you picked up, apparently you're not the only one.
Impressed. I've never seen anyone survive in Cola-Cars for more than a minute.
Of course, you did have a little help.
Gage was the one that clued you in. Yeah, I know all about it.
Don't worry, your secret is safe with me... I think Colter got exactly what he deserved.
I don't care why it happened, I'm just glad that it did.
As long as you run these gangs the way they were meant to be run, we'll never have a problem.
Heh... that's like calling a Deathclaw a pussycat.
Hell no. Colter was a goddamn idiot.
With Colter running the show, business was starting to dry up.
Now that you're here, I'm betting everyone stands to make a lot more money.
Speaking of which, I'm hoping you're here to spend a little bit of your own at the Nuka-Cade.
Yeah, it's pretty amazing, isn't it?
True, but that would be the stupidest decision you could ever make.
Thanks. It's taken me almost a year to get it this far.
It's my pride and joy. The one good thing that came out of getting stuck working for Colter.
All of the amusements and games around you are working thanks to yours truly.
I'm the only one that can take them apart and put them back together again. If I go, then it's only a matter of time before they fall apart.

Well then, step right up and I'll explain how you can win some.
All right, all right. No need to get hostile.
Give me a break, it's all I've got.
Look, let me just explain how all this works. After that, if you're interested in playing, great. If not, then no harm done.
Eager to play, eh? That's what I like to hear.
Every game in the Nuka-Cade still takes the original pre-war tokens. Just slot one into the machine and you're off.
If your score is high enough, the machine will kick out a bunch of tickets which you can redeem at a prize terminal for all sorts of goodies.

May as well start off with you on the right foot.

Hey, boss. Ready to spend some caps?

Well, some of the things in the redemption system are left over from the pre-war era.

You can win plenty of ammunition or even some special weapons and armor that I created myself.
If you're curious, go take a look at a redemption terminal. I'm betting you'll find something worth blowing your tokens on.
Otherwise I could always show you what I have for sale.

After a while, Colter starting considering me almost a part of the gangs. That's probably why he never slapped a collar around my neck.

Got plenty to play around here. Have at it.

Plenty of prizes still left in the redemption system.
Here to test out your skills, boss?

If I've got his ear, I can steer things in a direction that benefits me, and usually everyone else.
You ain't like Colter. That's what I'm getting at. You ain't like the other Raiders I've run with.

Okay, I mean, I was wondering...

That's what I mean. They don't get how it is. They don't see it coming.

When the Raiders came through, everyone rolled over. Raiders took what they wanted, moved on, and that was that.
Didn't take long before I finally figured out where I'd really been heading all along.

Connor's always coming to me, asking what I think of his plans, telling me how much he trusts me. Can't lie - it all went to my head.
After something like a year of working closely together goes by, we come up with this plan to make peace with a rival gang.
We work the whole thing out in secret. Where we meet on neutral ground, who does the negotiating, what happens if shit goes south.

I know, and if I'd been a bit smarter about it, maybe I'd have thought it was too much.

Brilliant. No wonder you're in charge.

My wife, Lisa, told me to run, so I did. Now I just got to hope it's not too late to save them.

My family and I ran into some traders a while back... told us they knew a safe settlement... at Nuka-World. But when we got there...
Found out they were Raiders the whole time, just stringing us along.
I managed to escape, but my wife and son are still back there. I wanted to get some help and go back for them, but didn't count on taking a bullet.
I can pay you. You bring them back safe, and I'll give you everything I have. Just hear me out.
Hell, I've seen better days, but it's my family I'm really worried about. Once those Raiders realize I'm gone...
I don't even want to think about what they'll do to them. God, you've got to help me.
I guess you'll just have to trust me. Not like I can prove myself one way or another.
I'd stay put. They'd know something's up if I went with. Does this mean you'll do it?
Just leave me. My wife and kid are all that matters, you hear me? Tell me you'll do this.
You're here for a reason. You've got to be.
I turned on a transmitter here, hoping someone would come. I didn't think it'd work, but here you are.
It's got to be a sign, right? So please, I'm begging you. They're running out of time.
If not for them, do it for the caps. You bring them back safe, I can pay you. I swear.
Here, take this. It's the password to the monorail control terminal.
Damn it. Please don't be another crazy.
It wasn't supposed to go down this way!
Hope you're packing plenty of ammo.
Find the control terminal. The power should be easy to restart.
Come on, that monorail ain't going to power itself. Get to the terminal.
Quit wasting time and get that power on.
I told you not to worry about me. Just find the terminal and get the power back on to get to Nuka-World.
What are you waiting for? Go find them.
You're still here? Move it, before it's too late!
Make sure you're ready to deal with those Raiders before you get on that monorail. They ain't no joke.
It's no small thing you're doing for me. I won't forget it.
I'll be keeping my fingers crossed for you.
Oh, thank God. The Raiders have my fam -- Oh, hey, boss.
I'll keep packing 'em in and the Gauntlet can keep picking 'em off.
Hey, I got you in there, didn't I? One way or another, I'll keep 'em coming.
Are you kidding? If I split, people I know get hurt. I can handle screwing over a stranger, but not people I care about.
Yeah, I guess it's all I'm good at. Since I fooled you, the gang leaders seem to think I've got some sort of special talent... suckers.
You... You're serious, aren't you? I ain't gonna say no, and believe me, it'll be a cold day in hell if you see me in the Commonwealth again.
You're a real ray of sunshine, boss. Guess I'll see you around.
Yeah, yeah. Maybe someday I'll learn that lesson, but for now, I guess I'm stuck here.
I'm just saying I'm tired. I'm down with doing whatever it takes to live to see another day.
So, you're the new boss? Colter was no pushover. If you were tough enough to take him out, then you definitely earned that title.
Whatever his faults, I'll say this - Colter built something here, something important.
A lot of people come a long way to trade here. A lot of caps flow through the market.
It's the kind of opportunity you don't want to take for granted.
Exactly. Customers means caps, and caps means everyone's happy. When a bunch of Raiders aren't happy any more, the bullets start flying.
Sorry, no offense Just trying to help. After all, if this place prospers, it's good for both of us.
As long as people feel safe coming here, the caps will flow. But Raiders, they're unpredictable. Quick-tempered.
It's a delicate balance, this place. Lot of reward but just as much risk. All I'm saying is, don't let things get out of hand.
There's one other thing you should probably know. I've got an arrangement with Shank.
When I'm out on the road, I keep an ear to the ground. I hear anything interesting, I relay it to him.
Your serious? Fine. I'll finish up my business here and hit the road.
Meantime, you better watch your back. People who make bad decisions don't last long around here.
Anything noteworthy happens either here in Nuka-World or out in the Commonwealth, odds are, he's well-informed about it.
Call it preferred status. I get first crack at whatever the gangs bring in. Helps me keep a good stock on hand.
Since you're here, can I hook you up with any supplies?
As long as you keep your eyes on the merchandise and off of me, I'm happy to do business.
Found some pretty good stuff while I was out on the road. Interested?
I've got all sorts of gear to choose from. Have a look.
It changes every day, to be honest, but I try to keep a good mix of weapons, supplies and trinkets. It's a decent bet I've got something you want.
I'll be hitting the road soon.
I'll be gone by the time you come back.
Word is Super Mutants just hit the place. They're still trying to rebuild. Should be easy pickings.
It's good, Shank. I don't bring you bullshit.
Shank, you'll be the first to know.
Please, just leave me alone. I'm doing my best, okay?
Can't believe you took on all three of those raider gangs. You must be the bravest person in the whole Commonwealth.
Hey there mister boss, sir. Lauren sells the drinks. You probably want to talk to her.
Hey there miss boss, ma'am. Lauren sells the drinks. You probably want to talk to her.
Working hard to keep the place nice and clean for you and the other Raiders, just like always.
Oh, look who it is! Always good to see the boss.
Never been much for conversation. I'm sure there's lots more interesting people to talk to around here than me.
Lauren's in charge around here. Well, I mean, she runs the bar. Obviously, you're the one in charge.
Doing my best to keep up with the mess. Not that I'm saying you're messy. Or any of the other Raiders. Just... yeah, I'll get back to work.
Hey! You shoot my help, and you're gonna be the one pushing that broom. You got that, tough guy?
I don't give a mole-rat's ass how many of you Raiders there are. In here, it's my bar an' my rules.
Was hoping you'd come by, boss. You ain't really seen the park 'til you've stopped in for a drink at Cappy's.
Trust? Hell no! A real raider don't trust anyone but himself.
Hah! Not bloody likely! I'd have to have a death wish to try a stunt like that.
Damn right! Hell, I'm an institution.
Go on now, take a guess why I ain't got a collar.
The answer is, because they got a healthy mix of fear an' respect, that's why.
That, an' the fact I don't take shit from anyone. In here, it's my drinks an' my rules.
You're right about that, haha.
Fine, I'll put the damn thing on. I'll even serve you a drink, but I'll still be hopin' you get a knife between the ribs from one of these Raiders.
Around here, backbone's the only thing that'll keep you alive.
You say "Raiders" like they're somethin' worse than normal folks.
I'll tell you what raiders are. They're people who see the true nature of the world, an' they ain't afraid of it.
Now what the hell do you want?
Now, what I can get you, boss?
I can see the question in your eyes. Why ain't she got a collar? You want to take a guess?
Really cleaned the Raiders out of here, didn't you? Too bad, they were some of my best customers. So, what are you having?
Strong man like you needs a strong drink, am I right?
Strong woman like you needs a strong drink, am I right?
Thirsty work, runnin' the park. Don't you worry, sweetheart, I got you covered.
Work hard, play hard. That's the raider way. So, what are you drinkin'?
Damn collar itches like the devil. Now, what do you need?
Can't stand wearin' this damn thing. What can I get you?
I got whatever liqour this rowdy bunch can get their hands on, from fine spirits to rotgut that'll put a fire in your belly. What'll it be?
I've got something... but I think you're going to need two doses.
Hmm. Yeah. Got something that should help.
And just... remember us when you're crushing those poor settlers under foot, huh?
Here. Ingredients for that one have been tricky to find, but between that and the grenade...
...you should have no problem getting at least one settlement under your control before you have to brew up your own.
So, the other trick up our sleeve is a little more broad spectrum than the grenades.
Gulp down a couple lung fulls and anyone you speak to will be far more apt to give you what you want.
Plus, I've winnowed down the addiction risk to near zero, so no need to fear repeat usage.
Here. Only had enough to make the one locally, but you can take the recipe as well, should you find yourself wanting more.
Nothing beyond that. I've been trying to get the formulas working better for more varied environments, but for now, the current brew should serve.
Just give them a try, first, Overboss. I think you'll enjoy the results.
Try before you judge, Overboss. I've had some... pleasing results in the past.
Well, this should give you one.
So, first up, something to manipulate a target. It's a homemade distillation brewed to mimic human flight pheromones.
Toss one these grenades at the feet of your target and convincing the wary settler suddenly becomes a much simpler affair.
Now, to function effectively, I had to make the formula... precise...
... so it's not going to work on people in any of the big towns like Bunker Hill or Diamond City nor on caravaners.
But for your purposes, hitting settlements out in the Commonwealth, it ought to give you a serious edge.
Would never dream of it, Overboss. I'm only hoping you remember which gang's help you're receiving.
I've put together a couple of toys that should make getting what you want out of those rubes easier.
Only thing we ask in return is that you remember the Operators when you're handing out new territory on the other side of the mountains.
Shank said you might need some tools to... ease relations with the people of the Commonwealth.
Well, you've come to the right place.
You looking for the boss? That'd be Mags.
Wasn't sure we could count on you. Good to know I was wrong.
Like how things are going, boss. We could get used to this kind of treatment.
Really making a place for yourself among Operators, aren't you?
You familiar with thallium, boss? Odorless, tasteless, colorless... and a very painful way to go.
If you're not going to support us, fine. Just don't hand this place over to the Pack. Turn it into one big litter box.
Oh. Our least favorite Overboss. Come back when you've claimed us some territory.
Colter's pushing up daisies. It's a lovely gift, but I think there are others our gang would like more. New territory, maybe?
There are some sick minds running around Nuka-World. All the more important you back us.
Underling to Overboss, Mags and William don't take well to being let down. So, make sure you get them something nice.
And here I thought this place was gaudy with the lights off. Still, not too shabby, boss.
Brought this place back to its former... is "glory" the word?
Our Overboss returns triumphant.
Would kill for an actual lab... though it hasn't helped yet.
Always thought the Parlor was a bit garish... but Mags and William have taken a shine to it and two trump one, I guess.
Walking around this place at night... even without the bodies it's hard to believe they ever let children in here.
Hmm. I wonder how much of Gage's little plan to replace Colter wasn't just to make sure the bosses didn't take off his head too?
So much money spent on this place. Just to hock some soda. Sad, don't you think?
Didn't think this through, did you?
Let's see what you're really made of.
So, this will be test eight of the persuasion formula. Our subject here... what'd you say your name was again?
Okay, we're just going to call you Eight. Eight, would you please stop talking?
As you can see, Eight is currently not receptive to my suggestions. Now, deploying the formula.
Hey yourself. Eight, how do you feel right now?
Excellent. Now, Eight, I'm going to untie your arm.
Now Eight, I want you to hit yourself.
Again, please. Harder this time.
That's perfect. Thank you, Eight. You can stop now.
You're welcome. Eight, I'm going to untie you. Now, if you wouldn't mind-- Eight! What're
Thank you, William. I-I'm going to need a minute, before you bring in number nine.
Oh! Uh... hello, boss! Congratulations on taking out Colter... I'm, uh... sure you'll do great.
Then this might be your chance to make some changes around here.
Okay, okay, I get it boss... just take it easy. There's no need to get angry.
Just forget I said anything, okay?
The gangs that call this place home are already at each other's throats.
Now you could risk your own neck and try to keep them from tearing each other apart or you could do the right thing and put them in the ground.
When it's all said and done, you're still running this place from your fancy house on the mountain over there.
Except now, you won't have to sleep with a gun under your pillow.
If you're serious about this, then you'll have to take down the leaders of the gangs.
That means putting a bullet into Nisha, Mason, Mags and Mags's brother, William.
Once you do that, the gangs will collapse and Nuka-Town goes back to being a free-trading post.
I should uh, probably stop talking about this out here. Just keep what I said in mind.
No offense intended. I know it's a tough job.
That's understandable, but whether you want it or not, the job is yours.
Well, to be honest, I don't know you yet, boss.
Sorry I'm so nervous, I don't mean any disrespect, boss. I just don't know where I stand with you and I don't want to end up pissing you off.
That, and, well... I'd be lying if I said we weren't all wondering how you plan to run the place.
I mean, this is your show now, you can do anything you want. You could run Nuka-World just like Colter... business as usual. Or...
You could uh, well, you know... get rid of the Raiders if you don't exactly agree with how they're running the place.
Of course, I understand. Still taking it all in.
Anyway, I'm the closest thing to a doctor around here. I treat everyone, Raider, trader and traveler alike.
Got some medical supplies, too, if you're looking for those.
Boss... I... I can't believe it. You killed the Raiders. All of them. I have to ask... why?
Can't argue with that. If you ask me, they've had it coming for a long time.
Oh... okay, boss. I, uh...  really hope you've scratched that itch now.
I guess not. I'm sure you had your reasons.
I've thought about this day for a long time. Dreamed about it, really, though I never thought I'd see that dream come true.
Now that it has, I'm left with a question that I don't know the answer to: What happens next?
Before you go, there's one last thing I need to say. Thank you. Sincerely, from all of us. Thank you.
Wherever the road takes you, know that you'll always be welcomed back here in Nuka-World.
Now if you'll excuse me, I've got to figure out a way to safely remove these collars.
Freedom... I never appreciated that word as much as I do right now.
You know, I think we should keep this market open. Bring it back to what it used to be, a place where everyone feels welcome.
You're right. We need to make that decision for ourselves.
I think we should keep this place going, like the way it used to be.
Only now, we can use the money we bring in to make our lives better.
Where do you feel this burning sensation?
Whoa, just stop there. I think I know what's wrong.
Here, take these, two every morning. Stop back in a week and we'll see how you're doing.
Good, hope it stays that way. Anything else?
What can I do for you? Treatment or supplies?
How are you feeling? I can take care of any sickness or injuries you might have.
Need me to look at you? Or just here for supplies?
Need some stims? Or I can patch you up myself.
Welcome. Dealing with any injuries or illnesses today?
Of course. What seems to be the trouble?
Alright, let's start with your symptoms. What's wrong, exactly?
Okay. What's troubling you, exactly?
Of course. Here's my current stock.
Well, it's a bit of a story. I used to live at a settlement west of here. We got hit by Colter and his gang, back when he had one.
It was a tough fight. We killed some of theirs, they killed some of ours. There were wounded on both sides, but nobody could get to them.
I called out for a cease fire on the condition that I'd see to their injuries as well as ours. Saved a few lives that day, including Colter's.
When he set up this place, he came back for me. Said if I came along, he'd make sure his guys wouldn't hit our settlement again.
Anyway, anything can I do for you? Treatment or supplies?
Hey, don't knock it 'til you try it, boss. I promise, you'll be glad you did.
The best mixers know that you can't achieve greatness without a little... imagination.
Can't exactly go out there with this collar on, but you can go anywhere you please.
I figure 10 hunks of Bloodworm Meat should do the trick. Then I'll show you just what kind of magic I can make.
There you go! Confidence is half the battle. You gotta make 'em believe it.
No doubt, no doubt. The trick is to keep them fighting something besides each other, right?
Yeah, long time ago. Got in a scrap and almost bought it. Decided the lifestyle wasn't worth dying for, so I got out.
Kicked around a few settlements trying to live straight. Got thrown out of em' all. Hard to change your ways, you know?
Came out here a few years back and was finally making it work. Then Colter and his gangs showed up.
Can't outrun your past, I guess. Sooner or later it's going to catch up with you.
So, think you're up to the task?
Yeah, but that was a long time ago, man. I won't waste your time with it.
So, you think you're up to the running this place?
So, you up for this? Ready to take charge and keep the peace between three of the nastiest raider gangs in the Wasteland?
Ain't gonna be easy, I can promise you that. I was a Raider once myself so I know how difficult they can be.
Well hey, I'm glad you stopped by. See, I'm the keeper of the sweet stuff around here. I've got chems for all your needs. But that's not all.
I've also got a proposition, just for you.
Alright, alright, I feel you. Gotta lay down that law, right? Let me come back at you another way.
I've been thinking about what kind of gift I can offer to show my appreciation for our new Overboss, and I know just the thing.
Not so, not so. I never touch the stuff anymore.
Now back in the day, did I indulge a little now and then? Sure, man. But I've turned the corner.
Now back to the matter at hand. I was about to make you a proposition.
Oh this is way better than caps, boss.
I've always had a gift for brewing chems. See, the trick is to experiment with your ingredients. Try everything, cause you never know, right?
The other day, I cooked up a real sweet blend of Calmex. This was solid gold, man. This was diamond dust.
Now I'd love to make some for you, being the big, bad boss that you are, but I'm gonna need a lot more Bloodworm Meat to do it.
Of course, of course. Got a kingdom to run, I understand. But if you change your mind, well, I'll be right here.
Of course, of course. No promises needed. Hey, this is your gig now. You do what you like.
Outside the park, mostly. They hide in the ground, until you get real close, that is.
So what do you think of my little proposal? We both stand to benefit, after all.
Now, what's your pleasure today? Any cravings I can satisfy?
Well now, what's this? Got us a celebrity here. It's the king of the castle himself. The one and only boss of Nuka-World!
Well now, what's this? Got us a celebrity here. It's the queen of the castle herself. The one and only boss of Nuka-World!
I'll get to work on that special Calmex blend soon. Shouldn't take too long. Check back with me next time you're in the market.
Now, what else can I get for you?
Hey, alright! Been gettin' acquainted with the local wildlife, huh?
Anything else you need today, boss?
The medicine you need to set yourself free. I've got chems of all kinds for you, boss. Just tell me what you need.
The boss comes callin'! What's your pleasure today?
Just the man I was hoping to see. Got something for you. I call it Calmex Silk, cause it's so damn smooth. Now, what else can I get for you?
Just the lady I was hoping to see. Got something for you. I call it Calmex Silk, cause it's so damn smooth. Now, what else can I get for you?
Ain't nothin' sweeter than freedom, boss. Gotta say, it feels pretty damn good. How can I be of service?
Hey hey, it's the baddest man in Nuka-Town! What can I get for you, my friend?
Hey hey, it's the baddest lady in Nuka-Town! What can I get for you, my friend?
Well, well, it's the highest and the mightiest himself! How can I be of service?
Well, well, it's the highest and the mightiest herself! How can I be of service?
Must be stressful, runnin' this den of backstabbers, throat-cutters and double-dealers. Let my chems put that troubled mind at ease.
Looking to chill out, or get fired up? Either way, I got what you need.
Stims, chems and other sweet treats. Get 'em while they last!
Feeling down? I got your pickup right here.
Take control of your mind! Fly without wings! I got chems for every need.
You're drifting away, looks like. I get that sometimes.
Sure do. Got years of practice perfecting my recipes.
I'd tell you more, but I can't go givin' up trade secrets. Got to keep that competitive advantage, you know what I mean?
But hey, there's more important things in life than being safe, right? What I can guarantee is an intense experience.
Overboss. Operators could use your skills if you've got some time.
Boss. Think you might be interested in taking on a task for us?
Hmm... you know, I've got a job set aside that I think you might find... stimulating... if you're interested.
Intriguing job came our way. It's yours if you want it.
You've got good timing. There's a job I think might benefit from someone with your... strengths.
If you're here looking to wreak a little havoc, I think I can provide. Up for some work?
Looking to get your hands dirty? You're in the right place. Job came across my lap. Yours if you want it.
You here to work? I've got something I think you might be able to handle.
Overboss. Got a task I think you might be a good fit for. Any chance you'd be up for some work?
Have some work, Overboss, that I think may be up your alley. Interested?
You know, boss, there is another task I think might pique your interest.
There's another job that could use your skills, boss. Would you want in?
Fresh opportunity for intrigue's become available, Overboss. Yours if you want it.
If you think you've got it in you, I've got another job available.
There is some more work available, if you're looking to take something on.
You know, the Operators could use you expertise again, if you're up for it.
There a chance you'd be interested in doing some more for us?
There is another job available, if you're interested, Overboss.
I did have another task set aside. Up for another round?
Operators have another... opportunity to offer, Overboss. Interested?
There's a local irritant they'd like to see dealt with. I want this irritant convinced to support our cause.
Someone's been leveling threats at them. I don't believe they pose any real danger, but I'd still like them to be taught the error of their ways.
Someone has been harrying our people. I'd like them to know what it feels likes.
Sounds like they're trying to make a new ally out in the wastes. I'd like the chance to present our case.
We've learned the identity of an... interesting party they've reached out to. Someone we'd like to have some words with.
Seems they've swayed themselves a dangerous ally. But if they could be swayed once...
There's a party I'd like to have a... frank discussion with.
We received a request to help someone learn some respect.
There's a favor I need done. An... old friend would like a rival taught a lesson.
Make her wear this Shock Collar. You can either sneak up to her and attach it or beat her into submission and then do it.
Apply this shock collar to her. If you can do it without being detected, wonderful. Otherwise, feel free to pummel her into agreement.
Make him wear this Shock Collar. You can either sneak up to him and attach it or beat him.
Apply this shock collar to him. If you can do it without being detected, wonderful. Otherwise feel free to pummel him into agreement.
Apparently one of them lost their nerve on a run, then fled without punishment. We can't permit that.
One of their own has decided to abandon our little family without permission. And I'm not one to ignore an opportunity to make an example.
Apparently one among them botched a job, fleeing the scene in shame. They can't be allowed to get away with that.
We've turned up the name of one of their fixers. I want them convinced we'd make better allies.
Someone has been passing them information. That information should be ours.
An independent party doing some work for us has accepted a job from them. I won't have that.
A contact of ours is looking to have someone punished. Publicly.
A request was made to break someone's spirits. No killing, though.
A mouthy trader out in the Commonwealth has been slandering the Operators. I'd like to see them shamed.
Seems they exposed a turncoat among them who fled before they could be apprehended. I want the traitor's head.
One of them was caught skimming from the gang's stores, then fled without punishment. No one steals from the Operators and lives.
Apparently one among them disobeyed an order, fleeing the scene to avoid the consequences of their actions. They need to be put down.
There's a trader who's been providing them with support. I want this toadie stopped. Permanently. Willing to do the deed?
They've managed to turn one of our fixers. I won't tolerate such betrayal. Eliminate them.
We've turned up information on one their dealers. I'd like this person put out of business. You'd be the one taking care of them.
A contact of ours is looking to have someone disposed of. You'd be the one putting them down.
Request came in that we remove a nuisance for a friend. They want them dealt with for good.
There's a loose end I'd like tied up out in the Commonwealth. I was hoping you could make sure no one ever hears from them again.
They've acquired a decent haul of supplies. Decent enough, unfortunately, to attract attention. That will need to be dealt with.
Sounds like they've come into something of a windfall. Their good fortune, though, hasn't gone unnoticed. These would be thieves have to be taken care of.
A caravan supplying them was hit. They're in need of help securing the stock.
Couple of our members uncovered a cache, but word has gotten out about their find. You'd head in to make sure we're the ones who end up controlling it.
A supply train of ours was hit, forced to dig in to protect the goods. I'd have you go in to ensure their survival.
Seems an exchange of goods hasn't gone over as planned. You'd be there to ensure our people come out on top.
They've collected information about a significant store of goods. Goods that should be ours. You'd help them collect.
They've turned up an opportunity to lay claim to a decent-sized cache. You'd make sure the prize ends up in our hands.
There's a collection of provisions they'd like to lay claim to. Unfortunately, someone else beat them to it. I want you to take it from its current owners.
There's a cache of supplies that would to better in our hands than its current owners. I'd like you to take care of it.
Group of scavengers stumbled upon a rich cache. Lucky for us, they blabbed about it. I want to make it ours.
There's some traders that refuse to sell to our people. I won't tolerate such disrespect. So you're going to take their goods.
We've become aware of a cache of supplies that I'd like to lay claim to... and it's current owners dealt with. Up to the task?
Some Super Mutants. Shouldn't be challenging.
A Super Mutant gang. Effectively target practice.
Raiders. Nothing but pushovers, really.
Brotherhood of Steel contingent. Certainly has the potential to be interesting.
Just some Brotherhood irritants.
A Minutemen contingent. No real danger.
Gunners. You'll want to watch yourself.
Gunner contingent. Chance for some real action.
Collection of Railroad agents. Should be easy pickings.
Some Railroad agents come up for air.
The Institute and some of their toy soldiers.
No one of consequence. I wouldn't overthink it.
Someone who's earned it. I wouldn't worry yourself much beyond that.
Overboss. Still game to close out that job for us?
Hope the task isn't giving you too much trouble, Overboss. You're still willing to take care of it?
Things proceeding with the job, Overboss? I can count on you taking care of it?
Then I'll let you get back to it.
Try not to delay too long. Makes us look... unfocused.
I guess you ought to get going then.
Good. The sooner it's done, the better.
One of our outposts could use some assistance.
An issue has arisen among the members of one of our outposts.
A request for support has come in from an outpost.
Populace at one of our outposts has reached out to us.
An outpost of ours is in need.
The Pack have managed to make themselves more irritating than usual.
The Pack continue to be a thorn in our side.
We have an issue with the Disciples.
The Disciples have made an annoyance of themselves yet again.
Then I won't waste any more of your time than I have to.
Well, then, let me get to the point.
Very well. Only thought you might value an opportunity to display your support.
Up to you, Overboss. I understand if it's all too much for you to handle.
Fine. Perhaps I'll find someone else, then.
Hmm. If that's what you'd prefer.
Then I guess we don't need to take up any more of each other's time.
I wouldn't want to burden you with something you're not equipped for.
Hmm. A shame. Thought I might be able to count on you.
If you're nervous, Overboss, I understand. Maybe someone else would be a better fit.
Inaction. I believe there was another Overboss who preferred that as well.
Your decision to make. Though I don't appreciate being left in the lurch.
Fine. If you can't decide, I'm sure I can find someone who can.

Sincerely? Yes. And I can tell you from experience, there's no use in fighting who you are.
